commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the OpenAI chat-tools E2E client to reject unsafe decimal `content-length` headers and adds a stalled-response regression test.

PR surface: Tests +23, Other 0. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. I reproduced current main with a local HTTP server sending `content-length: 9007199254740993` and no body; the client waited for its request timeout instead of failing on the body cap.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair job is needed because there are no actionable review findings; the draft maintainer PR needs validation and normal maintainer landing judgment.

target.title: fix(e2e): reject unsafe chat tools body lengths
target.body:
Summary:
- Reject unsafe decimal `content-length` values in the OpenAI chat-tools E2E client before reading response bodies.
- Add a regression covering a stalled response with `content-length: 9007199254740993` so it fails on the body cap instead of waiting for the request timeout.

Verification:
- `node --check scripts/e2e/lib/openai-chat-tools/client.mjs`
- direct local Node repro with a stalled local HTTP server and unsafe decimal content length; observed `chat completions response body exceeded 64 bytes` in ~121 ms and no timeout
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean

Notes:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/openai-chat-tools-client.test.ts` is blocked in this sparse GWT because `node_modules` is intentionally absent.
- A Testbox `pnpm check:changed` attempt queued `tbx_01kvgjvc86kjh3253km1xcck76` for ~3 minutes without starting; I cancelled it and the box was already stopped. That command briefly reconciled local `node_modules` before delegation; I removed the generated `node_modules` afterward.
target.author: vincentkoc
